title: Rényi divergences and binary state discrimination error exponents for fermionic quasi-free states
abstract: The trade-off relations between the two types of error probabilities in binary i.i.d. quantum state discrimination can be expressed by single-copy formulas in terms of the Petz-type and the sandwiched Rényi divergences of the two states representing the two hypotheses. In the non-i.i.d. setting, the error exponents can usually be expressed in terms of regularized Rényi divergences, which do not admit explicit formulas in general. Here, we consider a class of states, translation-invariant and gauge-invariant quasifree states on doubly infinite fermionic chains, and give explicit formulas for a wide range of regularized Rényi divergences between such states, including $(α,z)$, log-Euclidean, maximal, measured, and the recently introduced integral Rényi divergences. We show that the case where there is a single mode at each lattice site becomes asymptotically classical, with all the different types of regularized Rényi divergences being equal, while in the case of multiple modes per site, non-commutativity persists under regularization, and for any fixed $α$, the regularized Rényi $(α,z)$-divergences give different regularized values for different $z$ parameters in general. We also generalize a previous construction from [Bunth, Maróti, Mosonyi, Zimborás, Lett.~Math.~Phys.~113:(7), 2023] to the case of multiple modes per lattice site to obtain a large class of states exhibiting super-exponential decay of the discrimination error probabilities.
summary: This paper develops explicit formulas for various regularized Rényi divergences applicable to fermionic quasi-free states on infinite chains. By relating these divergences to the error exponents in quantum state discrimination, it provides a rigorous mathematical framework for quantifying distinguishability in complex quantum many-body systems.
main_problem: The paper quantifies the trade-off relations between error probabilities in binary quantum state discrimination, particularly for non-i.i.d. settings.
main_result: It derives explicit, closed-form formulas for various regularized Rényi divergences for a specific class of fermionic quasi-free states, establishing links between these divergences and the achievable error exponents.
method: The analysis heavily relies on extending Szegő-type limit theorems from the theory of block Toeplitz operators to calculate the thermodynamic limit of quantum divergences.
model_or_system: The system under study is a doubly infinite fermionic chain modeled by quasi-free states ($\omega_Q, \omega_R$) defined on the CAR-algebra. The states are characterized by single-particle symbols ($\hat{q}, \hat{r}$) acting on the torus.
key_observables: Error probabilities ($\epsilon_0, \epsilon_1$), error exponents ($d_r, s_r$), and various regularized Rényi divergences ($	ext{D}_{	ext{reg}}^{\alpha, \gamma}$).
important_parameters: The R\'enyi parameter $\alpha$, the internal dimension $d$ (number of modes per site), and the spectral properties of the symbols $\hat{q}(x)$ and $\hat{r}(x)$.
main_assumptions: The derivation requires the symbols $\hat{q}(x)$ and $\hat{r}(x)$ to be bounded away from 0 and 1, and for the symbols to be Lipschitz continuous on a non-degenerate interval.
paper_structure: The paper builds from defining various Rényi divergences for quasi-free states (using limit theorems) to deriving explicit formulas for regularized divergences, and finally applying these results to establish the asymptotic error exponents for state discrimination.
why_interesting: The explicit calculation of divergences in the thermodynamic limit for fermionic systems provides powerful tools to analyze quantum correlations and distinguishability in many-body quantum matter models.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
